sections.heading: Overview
sections.content: A 60-minute workshop introducing inflation, price level changes, and simple measures used in introductory economics.

content.statement:
By the end of this session you will be able to:
- explain what inflation means in everyday and economic terms
- compare CPI and GDP deflator at an introductory level
- interpret a simple inflation scenario for different households

content.title: Inflation concepts warm-up
content.purpose: Activate prior knowledge and agree a working definition of inflation.
content.learner_task:
Discuss in your group:
- What changed in the shop prices?
- Who is most affected?

scenarios.title: Scenario A — Grocery prices
scenarios.content: Bread rises from £1.20 to £1.50 over a year while wages are unchanged.

scenarios.title: Scenario B — Rent and energy
scenarios.content: Rent is fixed but energy bills increase sharply for a student household.

task_cards.title: Card 1 — Define inflation
task_cards.instruction: Write a one-sentence definition of inflation in your own words.

task_cards.title: Card 2 — Spot the price change
task_cards.instruction: List three prices in the scenario that increased and one that stayed the same.

content.expected_output: A shared definition plus one example from each scenario.
content.support_note: Circulate during minutes 5–12; prompt groups to use evidence from the scenarios.

content.title: Measures and impacts table
content.learner_task: Complete the table and answer the prompt set.

materials.impact_table:
| Measure | What it tracks | Limitation |
| --- | --- | --- |
| CPI | Consumer basket prices | Basket may not match your spending |
| GDP deflator | All domestically produced goods | Less intuitive for learners |

prompt_set.heading: Discussion prompts
prompts: Which measure would you report to a student newspaper?
Who might be harmed most by the scenarios?
What policy response is hinted at in the overview?

content.expected_output: Completed table rows plus short answers to two prompts.
content.support_note: Offer a worked example for CPI if groups stall in the first five minutes.

items.stem: Which situation best illustrates inflation rather than a one-off price change?
options: A general rise in many prices over time
A fall in quality of one good

limitations: Representative fixture for renderer regression tests.
